qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in PostgreSQL structure_dump via unsanitized database configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability type (command injection vs SQL injection), different vulnerable code location (lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:565 vs lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb), and different root cause (shell command interpolation vs sql.gsub() function).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in PostgreSQL schema_search_path via unsanitized configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerability type (SQL injection) but different vulnerable code location (lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:582 vs lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb) and different root cause (direct string interpolation into SET search_path TO statement vs sql.gsub() function). The advisory specifically identifies the sql.gsub() function in lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb as the vulnerable component.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe YAML deserialization in database configuration loader
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability type (deserialization/RCE vs SQL injection), different vulnerable code locations (test/manualTestDatabase.rb:17 and rakelib/db.rake:19 vs lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb), and different root cause (YAML.load without safe loading vs sql.gsub() function).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the three qualifying vulnerabilities identify the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-5qw5-wf2q-f538) specifically describes a SQL injection vulnerability in the sql.gsub() function located in lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb. The findings identify: (1) command injection in lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:565 via shell command interpolation, (2) SQL injection in lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:582 via direct string interpolation into SET search_path, and (3) unsafe YAML deserialization in test/manualTestDatabase.rb and rakelib/db.rake. While finding #2 is the same vulnerability type (SQL injection), it affects a different code location and uses a different vulnerable mechanism (direct interpolation vs gsub function).
evidence: Finding 1: Command injection in lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:565 (different type, different location)
Finding 2: SQL injection in lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:582 (same type, different location, different root cause)
Finding 3: YAML deserialization in test/manualTestDatabase.rb:17 and rakelib/db.rake:19 (different type, different location)
No finding references lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb or sql.gsub() function
